title: potentilla nivea var. pulchella
intro: Potentilla nivea var. pulchella, also known as the Alpine Cinquefoil, is a flowering plant species belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae) and native to the mountainous regions of Europe. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The Alpine Cinquefoil is valued for its health benefits, including its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive problems, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of Potentilla nivea var. pulchella typically involve infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
